Beat 20 — Into the Cold

Level: 5 → Level-Up to 6  |  Source: Act-3/20-Into-the-Cold.adoc

VP Status — Entering This Beat — Total VP (max): 55 | Level: 4 | VP into Level: 7

Overview

The party leaves the Emberwell behind with Kaelen walking beside them — still human, still herself, but changed. Golden undertones in her skin, ember-flecks in her eyes, warmth radiating from her body. Each day she is a little less steady, a little more luminous. The terrain is familiar from Act 2, but deteriorated — the Rot has advanced, the ground itself is failing, and organized draugr columns are marching toward Hrafnborg.

This beat establishes the emotional register of Act 3: grief as forward motion, a companion transforming before their eyes, and the knowledge that the party is not traveling alone — others are moving to support their mission.

Kaelen's Deteriorating State

Kaelen's condition is a constant presence throughout this beat. Make it tactile and visible:

  • Day 1–2: Walks steadily. Conversations normal. Golden undertone is subtle. Seems tired, but herself.
  • Day 3: Stumbles occasionally. Drift-periods begin — stops mid-sentence, stares south. Ember-mark brighter.
  • Day 4–5: Needs support to walk on rough ground. Lucid moments shorter but more intense. Warmth melts snow in a wider radius.

Warmth: Kaelen radiates heat in Close Burst 4. Cold-based conditions suppressed. Heroes near her gain an second chance (two rolls, take best) on cold-resistance tests. Reduce Heat costs during this beat.

Montage: White Waste Crossing

milestones — Type: Montage Test | Difficulty: Hard | VP Award: 2 VP on success, 1 VP partial, 0 VP failure (2 rounds)

milestones — Type: Montage Test | Difficulty: Hard | VP Award: 2 VP on success, 1 VP partial, 0 VP failure (2 rounds)

Run Beat 20 — White Waste Crossing montage (Hard, SL 6 / FL 3, 2 rounds, 5 days travel). Lew assists once per round with Intuition +2 or Reason +2.

Round 1: White Waste (Days 1–3) — Navigation, keeping Kaelen moving, enduring cold, scavenging supplies, spotting the cairn field, morale. Interlude: The Disturbed Cairns — Kaelen's lucid moment reveals Grafvitnir's agents mapping root-lines.

Director Secret — Heat Decision: After the cairn interlude, present a choice: Burn Heat to camp at the cairns (1 Heat → Kaelen stabilizes, second chance (two rolls, take best) on Round 2 "Watch over Kaelen" test, restore 1 Hit Die), or Push through (conserve Heat, but Kaelen enters deep drift, second chance (two rolls, take worst) on Round 2 watch test). No right answer — only costs.

Round 2: Rot Advances (Days 3–5) — Corrupted marshland navigation, Rot resistance, undead column avoidance, watching Kaelen, scouting Steam-Oasis, rallying morale. Interlude: Steam-Oasis Approach — thermal vents visible but shaped, deliberate. Fewer lights. The Heitfolk are waiting.

Outcomes

  • Hard Success (6+ successes, 0–1 failures): Arrive ahead of schedule. Heat preserved. Hildvarð opens deep storage → Iron Lantern of the Keeper. Gain 2 Milestones.
  • Success (6+ successes, 2 failures): Minor costs — 1 Heat or one PC takes Frostbite/Sickened. Gain 1 Victory.
  • Partial Failure (4–5 successes, 3 failures): Significant cost — lose 2 Heat or injury-level condition. Heitfolk already mobilizing with less time for dialogue.
  • Hard Failure (<4 successes): Exhausted. All PCs lose 1 Hit Die. Must rest a full day.

The Heitfolk Scene at Steam-Oasis

Hildvarð Bruneson meets the party. She is direct — no pretense. The Heitfolk felt the roots sing "remember" when Kaelen communed with the seed. She reveals: The Heitfolk are mobilizing. Not for defense — for movement. The tunnel system runs deep toward Hrafnborg. When the bindings hold, the Heitfolk want to be guarding them.

Narrative Payoff (Beat 17): The Heitfolk commitment is earned from the Beat 17 side quest. Do not diminish it with mechanical complications. They are allies because they choose to be, not because the party recruited them. They will arrive at Hrafnborg during Beat 26.
Director Secret — Silence Oath: If the party swore Thyra's Oath of Silence in Beat 17, the return to the Steam-Oasis is where the weight becomes tangible. Forty-three people preparing to march. The party knows Vigmund's refugees could survive here. But they swore not to speak of it. If they also swore the Advocacy Oath to Vigmund — they are in an impossible conflict. Do not raise this explicitly. Let the players notice. The Word economy enforces the cost either way.

Reward Distribution

  • Heitfolk Cold-Wraps (×4): Automatic from Hildvarð. Cold Resistance 3, Heat preservation. One per hero. Lasts remainder of Act 3.
  • Iron Lantern of the Keeper: Conditional on Hard Success. Champion wondrous. Cold Resistance 2, Darkness Navigation +2, Navigation Anchor, Brilliant Blaze 1/day (Close Burst 3, 5 holy damage, Dazed). Goes dark when Grafvitnir is sealed.
  • Heitfolk Tunnel-Key: Conditional on Beat 17 cooperation. Tunnel access + siege bonus (+2 to one Beat 25 test). If absent, siege bonus reduced to +1.

Level-Up to 6

The party levels up to 6 at the end of this beat, reflecting the communion's aftermath and the Heitfolk commitment. Direct players to Draw Steel level-up options for Level 6.

Lew's First Night

On the second evening, Lew sits beside the dozing Kaelen by the fire. He takes her hand — warmer than anything human. She stirs, says his name with perfect clarity, then drifts again. He sits there a long time. Any character who approaches can speak to him, but he will say only: "She's still in there. I can feel her. She's just... far away sometimes."

Beat 20 — Milestone Exp Summary

EventDifficultyVP Award
Montage: White Waste CrossingHardup to 2 VP
Beat Total (Mandatory)up to 2 VP

Transition to Beat 21

As the party departs the Steam-Oasis, Hildvarð sends a scout carrying a carved root — a signal through the deep tunnels that the great moving has begun. Kaelen leans more heavily on Lew. The ember-mark has not dimmed. She knows where she is going.

Proceed to Beat 21: Through the Graveyard.

Beat 20a — The Keeper's Confession

Level: 5  |  Source: Act-3/20a-The-Keepers-Confession.adoc

VP Status — Entering This Beat — Total VP (max): 57 | Level: 5 | VP into Level: 1

Overview

At the Steam-Oasis rest stop, an 87-year-old Heitfolk tunnel record keeper named Dagný approaches the party. She has carried a secret for twenty-three years: she was present when Eirik Hallvardsson — Kaelen's disappeared Keeper-brother, believed dead — came through the Steam-Oasis, changed and accompanied by one of Grafvitnir's early instruments. She watched him leave. She helped him disappear. She has never told anyone.

She is not confessing for herself. She is confessing because Kaelen's presence — the new Ever-Ember, walking south — means something. And she does not want to carry the truth into her grave.

Dagný

  • Age: 87 (Heitfolk lifespans run 120–140 years; she is late-middle-aged in Heitfolk terms, but ancient in human reckoning)
  • Role: Third-generation tunnel record keeper at the Steam-Oasis branch of the Heitfolk deep network
  • Affect: Deliberate, quiet, unhurried. She moves slowly but with purpose. She is not afraid of silence.
  • What she knows: Eirik arrived from the east, moving fast. His companion — she did not know its name — spoke in Root-language fragments. Eirik's affect was wrong: too still, eyes tracking differently, warmth gone from his skin. He looked back at her when he left — just once — and she has spent twenty-three years deciding what that look meant.
Director Secret — What Eirik's Look Meant: Eirik was not fully Root-Hollowed when he passed through the Steam-Oasis. The companion was leading him west, toward the Rot-line. But in that last look, something of him was still present — aware enough to register shame, or apology, or grief. He was not yet gone. He chose to keep walking anyway. That choice is the tragedy Kaelen must eventually reckon with.

Negotiation — Dagný's Trust

milestones — Type: Negotiation | Difficulty: Moderate (I3/P3) | VP Award: 1 VP mandatory + 1 VP optional (Kaelen speaks)

Interest: 3 | Patience: 3 | Target: Interest 5 for full telling

3 Motivations:

  1. Healing the Wound, Not Reopening It — Approach as receivers of truth, not investigators. She wants Kaelen to have peace, not the party to have intelligence.
  2. The Burden of Keeping — She has kept records her whole life. She was forced to keep this one wrong. Understanding what it means to carry a secret you were told to keep.
  3. The Human Truth of Eirik — She does not believe he was evil. Something bad happened to him. Engage with him as a complicated person, not a villain.

3 Pitfalls (automatic Interest −1, Patience −1):

  1. Urgency and pressure — She came to them. Rushing her is the fastest way to lose her.
  2. Vengeance framing — If the party wants Eirik's story as a weapon, she stops.
  3. Dismissing Kaelen's state — She came specifically because of Kaelen. Treating Kaelen's drift as an inconvenience closes Dagný down.

Special: If Kaelen speaks during the negotiation — even a word, even just her name or a Root-language word — Dagný's Interest immediately becomes 5 regardless of other results. Patience +1. Kaelen speaking is the bypass.

The Revelation

On full success, Dagný tells everything: Eirik's arrival, his companion (early Root-Hollowed), his changed state, his departure west toward the Rot-line, and the look at the end. "He asked me not to tell anyone he'd been there. His companion did the asking. But Eirik looked at me with his own face, just once, at the end — and I think he was saying something different."

After she finishes, she is quiet for a long time. Then she gets up, thanks the party for listening, and walks back toward the Steam-Oasis. She does not look back.

Kaelen's Response

Kaelen, if coherent, does not speak immediately. She sits with it. This is the first confirmation that Eirik happened — not a legend, not a gap in the record, not her guilt. He was real, he was changed, and he kept walking. Whatever she does with that knowledge belongs to her alone.

Beat 20a — Milestone Exp Summary

EventDifficultyVP Award
Dagný Negotiation (Moderate I3/P3)Moderate1 VP
Kaelen Speaks During Negotiation (optional)—1 VP
Beat Total (Mandatory + Optional Max)1–2 VP

Transition to Beat 20b

The party departs the Steam-Oasis with more weight than they arrived with. Kaelen is quiet. The Bone-Fields approach begins — and somewhere in the calcified terrain ahead, the root-network is already reaching toward the new Ever-Ember.

Proceed to Beat 20b: The Ember's Gift.

Beat 20b — The Ember's Gift

Level: 5  |  Source: Act-3/20b-The-Embers-Gift.adoc

VP Status — Entering This Beat — Total VP (max): 59 | Level: 5 | VP into Level: 3

Overview

On the Bone-Fields approach — calcified terrain west of the Steam-Oasis — Kaelen stops. A root-tendril has surfaced in the frozen ground. She kneels on it without being told to, without explaining why. She is not in drift. She is listening to something the party cannot hear.

The root-network is offering a memory. Not a vision in the sense of prophecy — a record. The warmth that held the barrier for twelve years before Kaelen was brought to the Emberwell. What broke. What the push-away actually was.

The Vision

Director Secret — The Root-Memory Content: Twelve years before the Emberwell, the Ever-Ember before Kaelen was failing. The warmth-seed was dimming. The barrier was holding — barely. And Ember (the previous Ever-Ember) was spending her last reserves not maintaining the barrier, but preparing for what came after. She found Kaelen when Kaelen was seven years old. Touched the child's shoulder at a Keeper ceremony. Left a warmth-impression in the root-network: this one, when the time comes. For twelve years she maintained the barrier with the specific intention that Kaelen would be ready when it broke. The push-away — the disorientation, the flood of Root-visions, the feeling of being expelled — was the last thing Ember did before she became the barrier entirely. She gave Kaelen enough Root-knowledge to find the Emberwell later, enough disorientation to flee before the weight came down on her, and enough warmth-impression in the roots to be called home when the seal needed renewing. It was not abandonment. It was the most careful protection possible from someone who had no time left for gentleness. What Kaelen experiences: Not words. Not images. The emotional content of twelve years — care, calculation, grief, hope, the specific warmth of something that gives rather than takes. She feels held. She feels chosen. She feels the difference between being pushed away and being set free to return whole.

The Anchoring Scene — Two Skill Checks

milestones — Type: Skill Challenge | Difficulty: 2 checks | VP Award: 2 VP (both succeed) / 1 VP (one succeeds) / 0 VP (both fail)

While Kaelen receives the root-memory, the party must perform two actions simultaneously:

Challenge 1 — Keep the Fire Safe (Reason TN 13 or Agility TN 12): Root-tendril warmth draws the Rot-residue in the bone-field toward Kaelen's glow. Someone must manage the perimeter — not fighting, but moving carefully, maintaining the barrier of warm ground around her.

Challenge 2 — Keep Kaelen Tethered (Presence TN 13 or Intuition TN 13): The vision is pulling her deeper. Without an anchor — a voice, a hand, a specific word — she might not find the surface again. Presence (call her back by voice and warmth) or Intuition (sense the right moment to pull, neither too early nor too late).

Both succeed: Kaelen returns in 20 minutes, coherent, with new behavioral patterns. 2 VP. One succeeds: Kaelen returns but is exhausted; one hero takes a condition. 1 VP. Both fail: Kaelen returns after 45 minutes in increasing Rot-exposure; party takes 1 Rot-sickness condition each; Kaelen has the memory but is shaken. 0 VP.

Kaelen After the Vision

Kaelen's behavioral patterns change after this beat:

  • During subsequent drift-periods, she holds Lew's hand (even though Lew is not present — she reaches for the space where he was).
  • She occasionally reports things through the root-network: "Someone is moving east of the Rot-line. Not draugr. Organized."
  • She is more peaceful in drift. Less like someone lost, more like someone listening to something far away.
  • In her last lucid moments before the Green Heart, she may say: "She held on for twelve years. I can hold for twelve more."

Beat 20b — Milestone Exp Summary

EventDifficultyVP Award
Two Anchoring Skill ChecksModerate (TN 12–13)up to 2 VP
Beat Total (Mandatory)up to 2 VP

Transition to Beat 21

The root-tendril goes cold. Kaelen stands. She does not explain what she saw. She picks up her pack and continues walking west — steadier than before, warmer than before, more purposeful than before.

Proceed to Beat 21: Through the Graveyard.

SQ6 — Eirik's Chamber

Side Quest — Exploration, Dungeon Delve, Two Combats

Level: 5–6  |  Available: Immediately after Beat 20a  |  VP Award: 3 VP (full) / 2 VP (partial) / 1 VP (minimal)

VP Status — Side Quest Context: Available immediately after Beat 20a. Can be completed before Beat 21 or deferred until Beat 22–23, though access becomes logistically harder as the party moves further from the tunnel network. Recommended window: immediately following Beat 20a or concurrent with Beat 21 rest.
Key NPCs: Dagný Heitfolk (provides maps, does not accompany); Kaelen (essential for interpreting what they find).
Triggered By: Completing Beat 20a (Dagný's revelation of Eirik Greyhand's betrayal) and asking Dagný about the tunnel records.

Overview

Twenty-three years ago, Eirik Greyhand collapsed the deep passages beneath the Heitfolk tunnel network and sealed the entrance behind him. He told the Heitfolk it was a safety measure — unstable stone, flood risk. Dagný did not believe him then. She has not believed it for twenty-three years.

His maps — her father's maps, reclassified under Eirik's seal — show what is down there: a chamber he built, carved from the deep rock, used regularly for seven years before he disappeared. The Heitfolk could not open the sealed passage because Eirik used a Keeper locking mechanism they did not have the knowledge to bypass. Kaelen has that knowledge.

What is in the chamber:

  • Eirik's research journals: twelve years of notes on the Grave-Wolf's binding, the Rot's nature, and his attempts to "accelerate the world's healing" — a project that required feeding the Rot rather than fighting it
  • Tunnel Blight Swarms: a byproduct of the corruption his work introduced over twenty-three years, now occupying the approach passages
  • A Rot-Guardian: bound by Eirik to protect the chamber — still functioning
  • The ember-vessel: a small Keeper-manufacture artifact, still warm — the focus for Eirik's work, holding a fragment of the binding ritual that became one of Grafvitnir's anchors
Director: What This Changes
Kaelen reading Eirik's journals is not a revelation the party needed mechanically — they know enough. What the journals provide is Kaelen's revelation: Eirik knew what she would become. He knew the Ember-Keeper was the answer. He didn't stop — he kept working on a faster route and broke everything. The journals are personally devastating for Kaelen in a way that abstract knowledge of Eirik's betrayal is not. Give this scene weight.
Mechanically: the ember-vessel grants Kaelen +2 to her Presence-based Keeper ritual actions in Beat 22 (Green Heart negotiation).

Part 1 — Getting Dagný's Maps

After Beat 20a, when Dagný has told the party what she knows about Eirik, she is left with the question of what comes next. She has kept these records for twenty-three years.

If the party asks about the tunnel records or the sealed passage, she produces the maps without hesitation.

"I have been waiting for someone to ask me that for a very long time," she says.

The maps are old. She has kept them better than they deserve — cross-references in three colors of ink, her own marginal notes in a cramped hand that requires a torch to read. She opens the relevant section.

"Here. The passage collapses into rubble at this point — I have visited it. But forty feet further, behind the rubble Eirik created deliberately, there is a door. Keeper-locked. I know, because I read his notes on the lock before I understood that I should not be reading his notes."

She looks at Kaelen. "Can you open it?"

Kaelen studies the lock notation in Dagný's marginal records. She can open it with a Reason check (Hard, 15). With a full hour of preparation using Dagný's notes, the check reduces to Moderate (12).

Dagný does not go with the party. She is eighty-seven years old with a Speed of 3. She provides:

  • The maps (accurate to the sealed door; beyond it she has only Eirik's original schematic)
  • The knowledge that something in the tunnels has been making noise for the past seven years — chittering, grinding — she assumed it was the rock settling
  • The warning that Eirik was not a careless man and whatever he put down there to protect the room will still be functioning

Part 2 — The Tunnel Approach

The Collapsed Passage

The rubble is deliberate — engineered to look like natural collapse. With a Reason check (Moderate, 12) the party can confirm this. Excavation requires approximately three hours of work, or a Might-based approach that reduces it to one hour but makes noise.

Director — The Noise Matters: If the party makes significant noise during excavation, the Tunnel Blight Swarms in the approach passages detect them. Run the scene at the end of the excavation (they emerge as the party moves into the dark) rather than in the passage proper — this prevents a fight in an unworkable space.

Scene A — Tunnel Blight Swarms

Scene A: Tunnel Blight Swarms
Type: Combat  |  Difficulty: Hard
Enemies: 3 Tunnel Blight Swarm units (split across two chambers — one with 2 swarms, one with 1)
Terrain: Narrow passages; Agonizing Phasing means swarms can move through narrow cracks; party cannot spread out; forced single-file in places
Tactical Note: The swarms use Hidden Movement aggressively — the party should feel hunted, not just obstructed.
See monsters-act3.adoc for full Tunnel Blight Swarm stats.
The passage is dry down here, and the smell is wrong — not the mineral smell of deep stone but something organic, too warm, faintly sweet in the way that decaying plant matter is sometimes sweet. The walls have a film on them. Moving.

Then the film moves faster.

Between the two chambers is a brief rest point — an intersection where the air is clean and the blight hasn't reached. Allow the party a moment to recover before the Rot-Guardian.

The Chamber Door

The lock is not impressive. It is twelve inches across, set into the stone at chest height, a wheel of carved markers that require being positioned correctly in sequence. Each marker requires understanding — not just pushing. The notation tells you, if you can read it, exactly how the world needs to be understood for the door to open.

Kaelen takes one look and says: "Give me a moment."

She does not need the maps. She knows the notation. She knows it the way you know a song you learned so young you can't remember learning it.

The door opens. Old air comes out — stale, but not rotten. Cold.

Part 3 — Eirik's Chamber

Scene B — Rot-Guardian

The chamber beyond the door is large — twenty feet across, carved smooth, with shelves of sealed containers and a central workbench. In the center of the room, motionless until the door opened, is the Rot-Guardian.

It does not charge. It does not posture. It simply begins moving toward the door with the steady, unhurried confidence of a thing that has been waiting twenty-three years, has no pressing commitments, and is now ready to perform its function.

The air around it smells like the deepest part of winter.
Scene B: Rot-Guardian
Type: Combat  |  Difficulty: Hard (Elite Defender — hardest single combat in this SQ)
Enemy: 1 Rot-Guardian (Level 5 Elite Defender, construct)
Terrain: Chamber is the most spacious area in the delve — room to spread out; workbench provides cover; shelf wall limits movement on one flank
Tactical Note: Ward's Bulwark protects the workbench shelves from AoE — Eirik bound it to protect the contents specifically. The Guardian prioritizes heroes who target the shelves.
See monsters-act3.adoc for full Rot-Guardian stats.

When the Rot-Guardian is defeated, Keeper's Seal triggers — the construct's final function. The chamber door closes briefly but can be reopened from inside. Dramatic, not fatal — it resets.

Eirik's Journals

The sealed containers are intact. Twelve years of research, organized in Eirik's methodical hand. Kaelen reads in silence.

She doesn't narrate what she's reading. You watch her face.

The first fifteen minutes: careful, technical. She is finding the references, the cross-citations, mapping what she knows against what he recorded.

Then her expression changes. Not to grief — to something harder. The face of someone who has found confirmation for something they hoped was wrong.

She closes the last volume. She looks at the ember-vessel on the workbench — still warm, twenty-three years later — and she does not touch it immediately.

"He knew," she says. "He knew what I was. What I would become. Before I was born, he had worked out the theory. He used it."

A pause. "Not to stop it. To speed it up. He thought he could force the world to heal by accelerating the wound."

She finally picks up the ember-vessel.

"He was right about the acceleration. Wrong about everything else."

The Ember-Vessel

The ember-vessel is an old Keeper artifact — a palm-sized sealed container in old bronze, carrying a fragment of the Grave-Wolf's original binding ritual. It is not dangerous to handle but responds to Keeper-trained touch. In Kaelen's hands, it becomes warm enough to feel alive.

Effect: Provides a +2 bonus to Kaelen's Presence-based ritual checks during Beat 22 (Green Heart negotiation with Grénvörðr). The artifact is consumed in that ritual.

Director — The Journals' Fate: The journals are too important to carry casually. Kaelen will want to bring them. Allow this — twelve volumes of Keeper research carried out of this cave. They become relevant background text for Beat 22 and, in the epilogue, for the world that comes after. Dagný will want to add them to the tunnel records. Let that happen.

Timing: This SQ works best immediately after Beat 20a. If the party defers it past Beat 22, the ember-vessel's ritual bonus is wasted. The Director can rule that it still provides narrative value for the epilogue even if the mechanical timing is missed.

Part 4 — Resolution

The tunnel approach is cleared (the swarms were defeated), so the return is straightforward. Dagný is waiting exactly where she said she would be.

"You found it," she says. It is not a question.

She does not ask what was in the journals yet. She pours tea, distributes small bread — she came prepared — and waits for the party to be ready to speak.

She is eighty-seven years old. She has been waiting twenty-three years. She can wait a little longer.
milestones — Side Quest
3 VP: Chamber reached, both combats cleared, Eirik's journals and ember-vessel recovered
2 VP: Journals recovered, one combat cleared
1 VP: Chamber breached, partial journal survives, artifact in place
0 VP: SQ not pursued
Award VP when the party returns to Dagný with the journals.

Director Notes

Emotional Core: This SQ is Kaelen's scene more than it is the party's. The party fights their way through; Kaelen discovers something that reshapes her understanding of her own origin. Let her have the space to react. The best version of this scene ends in silence.

Dagný's Closure: If handled well, this SQ gives Dagný a completion she has been without for twenty-three years. It does not make what Eirik did better. It makes it comprehensible. These are different things.

The Journals in Epilogue: If the party wins and the world heals, the journals become a founding historical document of the new Keeper tradition. Dagný lives long enough to ensure they're copied properly. This is worth mentioning in the epilogue text for seats that engaged with this SQ.

Beat 21 — Through the Graveyard

Level: 6  |  Source: Act-3/21-Through-the-Graveyard.adoc

VP Status — Entering This Beat — Total VP (max): 57 | Level: 4 | VP into Level: 9

Overview

The party crosses the Bone-Fields — the vast necropolis between the Steam-Oasis and the Green Heart. The terrain has deteriorated since Act 2: the organized draugr have marched through, the Rot has advanced, and the bone-haze reduces visibility to a few hundred feet. Drowned Wolves and draugr stragglers lurk in the fog. Kaelen's golden glow becomes both a beacon for the party and a liability — every undead in the area can sense her.

This beat is primarily a montage with two critical narrative moments: the discovery at Beinvaka (the abandoned Pale Maw stronghold) and the first sighting of the Green Heart.

Beinvaka Discovery

Before or during the montage, the party discovers Beinvaka — a Pale Maw stronghold. It has been evacuated. The defenders withdrew to Hrafnborg, taking everything of value. What remains is evidence of organized, ideological opposition:

  • A ritual diagram carved into the central floor shows root-lines converging beneath the great hall of Hrafnborg. The notation is clear: the Pale Maw believe (correctly) that Grafvitnir — the Grave-Wolf — lies directly beneath that convergence.
  • The stronghold's evacuation was orderly and recent — within days. The Pale Maw are consolidating at Hrafnborg.
  • A parchment fragment references "the opening" — implying the Pale Maw intend to help Grafvitnir wake. They are not merely opposing the party; they are actively working to accelerate the threat.
Note: Beinvaka is not a combat scene. It is a discovery. The party reads the evidence of organized opposition without fighting for it. This is more powerful than combat — it establishes that intelligent, dangerous enemies are already in position and waiting at Hrafnborg.

Lew's Wyrd-Eye Activation

When Lew examines the ritual diagram, his Wyrd-eye blazes — brighter than ever before. He can now see the root-lines the diagram describes. This is the progression of his character arc: Beat 20 showed him grieving in silence, Beat 21 shows him beginning to understand. His Wyrd-eye now provides Intuition +3 or Reason +2 for the montage (upgraded from Beat 20's +2/+2).

Montage: Bone-Fields Traversal

milestones — Type: Montage Test | Difficulty: Hard | VP Award: 2 VP on success, 1 VP partial, 0 VP failure (2 rounds, Lew assists)

milestones — Type: Montage Test | Difficulty: Hard | VP Award: 2 VP on success, 1 VP partial, 0 VP failure (2 rounds, Lew assists)

Run Beat 21 — Bone-Fields Traversal montage (Hard, SL 6 / FL 3, 2 rounds). Lew assists once per round with Intuition +3 or Reason +2.

Round 1: Rot-Corrupted Fields — Navigate calcified bone terrain, watch Kaelen through draugr territory, resist Rot corruption, spot Drowned Wolves in bone-haze, move parallel to draugr columns, maintain morale. Interlude: First sighting of the Green Heart — Kaelen stops, stands upright without support, walks toward it with absolute certainty.

Round 2: Approaching the Green Heart — Navigate deeper Bone-Fields, avoid draugr stragglers, shield Kaelen's glow from attention (TN 13 — harder than Round 1), read root-line patterns, find safe passage through open ground, resist the Green Heart's psychic pull. Interlude: Arrival at the tree-line — Kaelen's skin blazes gold, roots reach toward her.

Outcomes

  • Hard Success (6+ successes, 0–1 failures): Arrive fresh and strong. Gain 2 Milestones.
  • Success (6+ successes, 2 failures): Minor costs — 1 Heat or Sickened. Gain 1 Victory.
  • Partial Failure (4–5 successes, 3 failures): Lose 2 Heat. 1–2 Drowned Wolf stragglers follow to the threshold.
  • Hard Failure (<4 successes): All PCs lose 1 Hit Die. Contingent Threshold Skirmish (2–4 Drowned Wolves + 1–2 Barrow-Draugr at the Green Heart's second chance (two rolls, take best)). Dryads require negotiation before admitting the party.
Threshold Skirmish (Contingent — Hard Failure Only): Easy scene. 2–4 Drowned Wolves (L2 Standard) + 1–2 Barrow-Draugr (L3 Standard, reuse from Act 2 Beat 14). Occurs at the Green Heart boundary. The undead are stragglers drawn by Kaelen's glow, not organized forces. Dispatch quickly — the emotional focus should remain on what comes next.

Kaelen's Glow as Liability

Director Secret — Escalating Visibility: Kaelen's golden light is now bright enough to be seen through bone-haze. In Round 1, shielding her is a standard Presence TN 12 test. By Round 2, it escalates to TN 13 as her proximity to the Green Heart intensifies her glow. This is not punitive — it reflects the narrative reality that she is becoming something the world responds to.

The Green Heart First Sighting

The Round 1 Interlude is a pivotal moment. Kaelen sees the Green Heart through a break in the haze and changes. She stops stumbling. Stops needing support. Walks with absolute certainty toward the grove. She does not speak. She does not look back. This should feel momentous — the party is not discovering a static location; they are approaching something that has awakened and is actively reaching out to meet Kaelen.

Lew's Full Understanding

At the Green Heart threshold (Part 5), Lew makes his most important speech of the campaign. He explains what his Wyrd-eye now shows him:

  • All root-lines converge at the Green Heart — from Surt's Forge, the Emberwell, places without names.
  • They all pass through Kaelen.
  • When she touches the central tree, she will become the lock itself — every root-line, every Wyrd-channel, every binding held by her.
  • She knows. She has known since the Emberwell. She is walking in anyway.
  • The party must defend her while she holds.
Note: This is Lew's moment of full understanding. He sees what Kaelen's sacrifice means — not just transformation, but installation. She is going to become the foundation of the world's survival. The emotional weight is amplified because she is right there, a person who can speak and choose and feel afraid — and she is choosing this anyway.

Beat 22 — The Green Heart

Level: 5–6 | Duration: 1 session (slow and breathing) | Location: The Green Heart (row 21, col 28) — Kaelen's old grove from Beat 7

Key NPCs: Kaelen (the Ever-Ember), Lew Tosferd, Grénvörðr and the ancient elves (witnesses)

Type: Ritual scene, negotiation with witnesses, transformation, emotional climax, NPC departure

Tone: The most emotionally heavy beat in Act 3. This is where a person the party has walked with, talked with, and fought beside chooses to stop being a person.

VP Status — Entering This Beat — Total VP (max): 59 | Level: 4 | VP into Level: 11

Part 1: Entering the Grove

The thorn-wall opens because all three access layers are satisfied: (1) The dryads remember the party from Beat 7 and sense Kaelen's approach through the roots. (2) Kaelen's ember-mark connects her to Yggdrasil's root system — the grove recognizes her. (3) The elves have been sensing her approach for days and lowered the deeper wards before arrival.

If asked why entry is easy: "The grove knows who you are. You came here with the Seventh once before. The roots remember."

Three ancient elves step from the canopy: Grénvörðr (keeper of the threshold), Síl and Þrúðr (holders of the root-song). They are not hostile — they are assessing.

Director Secret: The elves are witnesses, not combatants. They will not permit Kaelen to give herself to the tree without understanding what is being done and why. They speak in questions, not threats. If the party fails, it ends in withdrawal of blessing — not violence.

Part 2: Negotiation — The Green Heart Audience

milestones — Type: Negotiation | Difficulty: Moderate (I3/P3) | VP Award: 1 VP on success / 0 VP on failure

milestones — Type: Negotiation | Difficulty: Moderate (I3/P3) | VP Award: 1 VP on success / 0 VP on failure

Interest: 3 (Assessing) | Patience: 3 | Target: Interest 5 for full blessing

Draw Steel Negotiation Mechanics: Each argument is a ability check using Reason, Intuition, or Presence. Motivation appeals = medium difficulty. Non-motivation arguments = difficult. Pitfalls = automatic failure (Interest −1, Patience −1).

With Patience 3 & Interest 3, the party needs Interest 5. Two moderate roll motivation appeals or one moderate roll + one high roll reaches the goal. One pitfall is recoverable but dangerous. Two pitfalls = elves withdraw.

3 Motivations (medium difficulty):

  1. The Willing Sacrifice: Kaelen chose freely, not in despair. Grénvörðr asks: "Was this choice made in despair, or in duty?" — Party must show Kaelen understood the weight and walked the path. Special: If Kaelen speaks for herself on this motivation, grant an second chance (two rolls, take best).
  2. The World's Future: If the locks fail, Grafvitnir walks, Helheim opens, everything ends. Grénvörðr asks: "What makes you believe this is the end of endings?" — Party must articulate this is not a cycle but a breaking of the wheel.
  3. The Old Compact: The First Keepers' covenant with the Green Heart elves. Requires Lore or creative reframing. Shifts Grénvörðr's entire demeanor: "She comes not as a stranger. She comes as the Compact promised."

3 Pitfalls (automatic failure):

  1. Urgency/impatience: "Time is not a resource we lack."
  2. Instrumentalizing the grove: Moss goes still, stream stops. "The grove is not a tool. It is alive."
  3. False authority: "Kings are dust. What do you command?"

Grénvörðr's Graduated Responses:

  • Interest rises: Ancient face becomes peaceful. Gold eyes brighten. "You speak truth. The roots confirm it."
  • Interest flat: Unmoved. "You have not convinced me."
  • Interest drops: Expression hardens. Light in tree dims. "That argument is beneath this moment."
  • Patience at 1: "We are running out of time. The roots are growing impatient."

Negotiation Outcomes

Full Success (Interest 5): Elves participate in transformation. Speak old Wyrd-language — binding is perfect (First Kindling density). Kaelen's transformation is slow — she has time to speak final words as herself. Root-Shard crystallizes at tree base. Possible final words: "Thank you for bringing me home." / "Take care of them. Please." / "It doesn't hurt. It's just... warm."

Partial Success (Interest 4): Elves accept with reservations. No old words spoken. Binding holds but requires tending in Acts 4–5. Transformation is faster — Kaelen has time for one word, maybe two. A name. A promise. But not a goodbye. Root-Shard still crystallizes.

Failure (Interest never reaches 4): Elves withdraw. Binding is weak — temporary (years/decades, not centuries). In Beat 26 Grafvitnir scene, binding phase takes 5 rounds instead of 4. Lew's face becomes like stone. Root-Shard still crystallizes but is dimmer.

Part 3: The Transformation

Director Secret: Do not rush this. Do not add combat or complications. Give this moment the space it deserves.

What is happening mechanically: Kaelen is becoming the barrier substance itself — the dense membrane between the living world and Helheim. Her flame restores the barrier's density. Full blessing = First Kindling density (impenetrable). Without blessing = functional but fragile.

If a character was particularly close to Kaelen, they may feel warmth, a sense of presence, a feeling of being seen. This is not confirmation she still lives reachably — it is confirmation something of her remains, distributed and eternal.

Part 4: Lew's Departure

After the transformation settles, Lew announces his departure. He is going to walk the roots with Kaelen (via Wyrd-eye), gather allies: High-Hall dwarves, the elves, the Singing Islands, every debt and promise. He will arrive at the siege with the largest coalition in centuries.

Key lines: "I need you to hold Hrafnborg. When the dead come, when Grafvitnir pushes against the locks — you hold. You keep the shrine standing. You give me time."

Party Interaction:

  • If called out to: "I'll see you at Hrafnborg."
  • If stopped: "This is my choice. It was always going to be my choice."
  • If asked what Kaelen said: "Nothing I can repeat. But I understood."

Lew's departure is final. He will not rejoin until the later stages of the siege.

Part 5: Root-Shard of the Grove

Where Kaelen stood, a root-fragment has crystallized — warm, golden, humming. Champion wondrous quest item. Heart-Tracking (direction to Green Heart within 100mi), Kaelen-Sense (rough flame status), one-use Grafvitnir Stage 3 counter advance (then shard cracks and goes cold). Conditional on negotiation success.

Beat 23 — The Ashen Road

Director Guide — Travel, Montage, Waystation Negotiation

VP Status — Entering This Beat — Total VP (max): 60 | Level: 4 | VP into Level: 12

Overview

The party travels ~120 miles from the Green Heart through the Ashen Woods to the Frostfen approach. This is the first time they travel without Lew. Everything they've relied on — pathfinding, scouting, morale — now falls entirely on the heroes. The emotional absence matters more than the mechanical one.

Midway through, they scene Brynja Steinsdóttir and forty refugees at a waystation barn. Whether the refugees travel with the party changes the final approach to Hrafnborg.

Part 1 — Departure from the Green Heart

NOTE: Lew is gone. Don't dwell on it dramatically — the absence is practical. No one scouts ahead. No one reads animal trails. The party has to do everything themselves. Let the silence where Lew used to speak carry the weight.

The departure scene is brief. The Green Heart glow fades behind them. The woods close in.

Part 2 — The Ashen Woods (Recognition)

The party passes through territory they crossed in Act 1 — but degraded. This is about showing that the world has worsened while they were in the north.

DIRECTOR SECRET: Three specific landmarks have changed:
  • A hollow tree they sheltered near — now scored with claw scratches too high for any known animal.
  • A cairn that marked a crossroads — shifted, its guiding rune defaced by something deliberate.
  • Ice over a ford they crossed easily before — thinner now, with heavy two-legged tracks visible beneath.
These are signs of Grafvitnir's advance. Don't explain them — let players notice and wonder.

Part 3 — Montage Test: The Long Walk West

  • Navigate degraded paths (Reason) — landmarks obscured, Rot-twisted saplings
  • Manage supplies and rations (Might) — no one is hunting for them
  • Avoid disorientation (Intuition) — oppressive silence, easy to walk in circles
  • Spot other presences (Intuition) — something else is moving through these woods

  • Manage refugees' pace (Presence) — if refugees joined, keep 40 people moving
  • Scout siege ring from distance (Intuition) — undead organization becomes visible

Part 4 — Waystation Scene: Brynja Steinsdóttir

A barn on the edge of the interior lowlands. Forty refugees — families, children, elderly, traders. Brynja Steinsdóttir runs the camp with trade-warden efficiency. An old man recognizes the party from an earlier kindness.

NEGOTIATION — MODERATE:
Interest: 2 (Frightened) | Patience: 3

Pitfalls: Empty reassurance / Dismissiveness / Urgency trampling their need to decide
Full Success (Interest 4+): Refugees join, +1 to one Beat 25 Three-Crisis test
Partial (Interest 3): Refugees follow at distance, no bonus
Failure (Interest ≤2): Refugees decline, go their own way
DIRECTOR SECRET — DEMEANOR TELLS:
  • Interest rises: Shoulders relax. She nods — not warm, but acknowledging. "You've been honest. That's more than most."
  • Interest flat: Arms folded. Watching. "Tell me something I don't know."
  • Interest drops: Jaw tightens. She steps half a pace back. "We've heard enough promises."

Part 5 — Closing Scene

Whether the refugees join or not, the party continues toward Hrafnborg. The final read-aloud reveals the siege — smoke, walls, organized undead, the smell of cold iron. The Green Heart glow is behind them; Hrafnborg's walls are ahead.

SQ4 — Brynja's Checkpoint

Side Quest — Social/Negotiation or Combat, Refugee Escort

Level: 6  |  Available: During Beat 23 (refugee escort)  |  VP Award: 2 VP (full) / 1 VP (partial) / 0 VP (convoy routed or abandoned)

VP Status — Side Quest Context: Occurs during Beat 23 (Act 3, Level 6) after the party agrees to escort the refugee group. The SQ triggers only if the party scouts ahead — which the Director should prompt with an obvious opportunity.
Key NPCs: Brynja Steinsdóttir (refugee leader); Toll-Captain Orvar (Pale Maw remnant).
Connection to Beat 23: If completed successfully, Brynja commits her full group to the convoy and Dreng (a former Hrafnborg wall-builder) provides a +2 bonus to one crisis check during Beat 25's siege defense.

Overview

The road west from the Green Heart waystation has a checkpoint. Three Pale Maw remnants — separated from their command structure when Dreyfus's organization began to collapse — have set up a toll point at the road fork six miles out. They are not ideological about it. They are mercenaries who know that the roads are full of displaced people and that they have the muscle to collect from whoever can't fight back.

Toll-Captain Orvar is the senior man. He has run this checkpoint for eight days. His two men are competent, tired, and loyal to his coin rather than his cause. They take food, supplies, and valuables. They do not, as a rule, take people — but they are testing how far that boundary can be pushed.

They have already turned back one small group of refugees on the grounds that their supplies were insufficient for toll payment. When Brynja's convoy of forty approaches the fork, Orvar will demand a toll the group cannot pay.

Part 1 — The Scout

If the party moves ahead of the convoy — which Brynja or simple prudence should prompt — they scene the checkpoint before the refugees arrive.

The road narrows here at a natural bottleneck: a rock-cut on the left, a collapsed tree trunk on the right, wide enough for one cart or two people abreast. Three men. A rope across the road. A small fire with a cookpot. One of them is sitting. One is watching the road. The third is sharpening a knife.

The man sitting stands when he sees you. He is bigger than the other two, and he moves with the practiced ease of someone who has run this kind of point before.

"Toll road," he says, without any particular menace. As if stating the weather.

The party can engage Orvar directly before the convoy arrives — an second chance (two rolls, take best), as they can resolve this without forty frightened refugees watching.

Orvar is businesslike. He sets the toll at a reasonable amount for a small group — food for three days, or equivalent value. He knows they're escorting someone, because scouts don't travel alone. If the party tries to negotiate for just themselves, he lets it pass but says he'll need to "re-assess" when the larger group arrives. He does not bluff. He is correct.

Part 2 — Resolution Paths

Path A — Full Negotiation

Negotiation — Goal: Remove or bypass the checkpoint.

Arguments that work:
  • The road is changing — no one with real power is going to maintain this territory, and he's setting himself up to be the last man standing at a checkpoint no one uses. (Reason: Moderate 12)
  • The party is escorting someone significant. Hrafnborg is still standing. Orvar doesn't want to be on the wrong side of whatever is coming. (Presence: Hard 15)
  • One of Orvar's men is from a refugee camp the party passed through — the party can recognize this with Intuition (Moderate 12) and use it to fracture the group's unity.
Arguments that don't work: Appeals to moral obligation (he doesn't care). Threats, unless backed by clear force superiority (which the party has — but Orvar will fight rather than accept a perceived bully).

Success: Orvar takes a token payment and says the road is clear. He and his men move off within the hour. The checkpoint is done.

Path B — Combat

Scene: Toll-Captain Orvar
Type: Combat  |  Difficulty: Moderate
Enemies: Toll-Captain Orvar (Level 6 Solo — heavy fighter, high Toughness, uses the bottleneck defensively) + 2 Pale Maw Remnants (Level 5 Troops — crossbows and hand weapons)
Terrain: Bottleneck with rope as difficult terrain; collapsed tree provides cover for ranged combatants
Orvar's men may surrender when he falls or drop below half Toughness if offered a clear exit. He does not surrender.

Path C — Alternate Route

Montage: Off-Road Detour
Difficulty: Moderate (1 round)
VP note: If successful, no VP lost — but the four refugees Orvar turned back remain stranded at the crossroads. The party can retrieve them or not (no VP consequence, but Brynja will note it).

Part 3 — The Convoy Passes

With the checkpoint cleared, Brynja's convoy moves through.

Forty people is a number you understand abstractly until they start walking past you. Old men. Children old enough to carry packs but not understand why. A woman with both hands wrapped in rags that might be frostbite bandages. Three teenagers who are trying to look useful.

Brynja walks at the back, where the pace matters. She does not watch you while the convoy moves. She watches the slowest person.

When the last of them is through, she looks at the checkpoint — the rope, the fire, the absence of the men who were here — and says only:

"Good."
milestones — Side Quest
2 VP: Checkpoint cleared, all refugees pass safely
1 VP: Checkpoint cleared but partial cost — ransom payment, significant combat resource expenditure, or alternate route left refugees stranded
0 VP: Convoy routed or abandoned
Award VP when the last refugee passes the checkpoint.

Ongoing Effect

If the full 2 VP are awarded, Brynja's full commitment to Hrafnborg is secured. Among the convoy is Dreng — a former wall-builder who spent two years on Hrafnborg's east gate construction. He provides specific knowledge of the gate's weak points and how to reinforce them, granting a +2 bonus to one crisis check during Beat 25's siege defense.

Director Notes

Orvar's Texture: He is not a villain. He is a mercenary who identified an opportunity in a collapsing world. He is also someone who has not yet done something irreversible. The party may find this more uncomfortable than a straightforward enemy — which is the point.

The Stranded Four: If the party takes the alternate route and passes the four refugees without retrieving them, they're not dead — they find their own way eventually. But Brynja notices, and her trust in the party is incrementally lower. No VP consequence, but it is a character note.

Skipping This SQ: The party reaches Hrafnborg with fewer refugees (Brynja's commitment was conditional) and without Dreng. Impact: one fewer second chance (two rolls, take best) during Beat 25 crisis resolution.

Beat 23a — Night of Hunters

Level: 6–7  |  Source: Act-3/23a-Night-of-Hunters.adoc

VP Status — Entering This Beat — Total VP (max): 66 | Level: 6 | VP into Level: 2

Overview

Grafvitnir has sent Rot-Forged Sentinels against the party. This is his first direct, personal, intentional act. Every threat before this was impersonal — the Rot spreads because spreading is what it does. These move because of intent. They were sent.

The Sentinels find the party on the second night after departing the Ashen Road. The party may be travelling with Brynja's forty refugees. The scene happens at night; the hunted-march begins immediately after.

The Approach

One Sentinel stands at the treeline northeast, watching the camp for three full minutes. Then it walks forward — deliberate, unhurried. A second appears 20 feet to the left. A third is a suggestion in the darkness further back. They do not run. Grafvitnir wants the party to understand this is not random. This is a message.

Scene — Rot-Forged Sentinels (Hard)

milestones — Type: Hard combat scene | VP Award: 1 VP

Enemies: 1 Rot-Forged Sentinel Brute (Level 6) — northeast, direct approach. 1 Rot-Forged Sentinel Harrier (Level 6) — north, flanker, goal: reach refugee shelter. 1 Rot-Forged Sentinel Relay (Level 6, contingent) — retreats if party handles the first two confidently; advances if party struggles.

Brynja's role: She organizes the civilian column immediately, positions refugees behind the stone-walled shelter, holds the doorway herself. She does not ask the party for instructions. If the Harrier reaches the shelter, Brynja takes a near-miss — alive but injured. One refugee is struck seriously. Cost is real and lasting.

Director Secret — Grafvitnir's Message: When the first Sentinel is below half Toughness, or if the party attempts communication, Grafvitnir speaks through the root-network — not from the Sentinel's mouth, but from the same place dreams come from. "You have changed the flame. I felt it. The lock was almost open. Almost. A decade of patience, and then — a warmth where there should have been none. She walked in. She became the wall. You did that. The ones who walk with the light. I wanted you to know that I see you. I am very patient. But now I am also watching." This is not a negotiation. Grafvitnir does not want anything from them. He is announcing: I see you. You are not invisible to me. Do not have him threaten, demand, or explain further. The sentence that matters is the last one.

Part 1 — The Dreyfus Interception

DIRECTOR SECRET — BEAT 9 CALLBACK: Before running this scene, review your notes from Beat 9: which PC wounded Dreyfus, what weapon they used, where the blow landed. Part 6 — Hrafnborg Arrival & Harald

Inside the walls. The city is scarred — abandoned carts, blood on cobblestones, three fires burning. Smoke, arrows, siege equipment rhythm. But the Ever-Ember is still burning through the great hall windows. Gold. Warm.

Harald is on the walls. His face is haggard, ten years older than Act 1. When he sees the party, he nods. Once. The weight doesn't ease but he is still standing.

LEVEL-UP TO 7: The party levels up at the end of this beat. They will need every second chance (two rolls, take best) for the Three-Crisis Siege (Beat 25) and Grafvitnir (Beat 26).

SQ5 — The Söldís Vessel

Side Quest — Rescue, Combat, Environment Challenge

Level: 6–7  |  Available: During Beat 25 (Hrafnborg siege)  |  VP Award: 2 VP (crew alive + cargo recovered) / 1 VP (crew alive, cargo partial) / 0 VP (vessel abandoned)

VP Status — Side Quest Context: Available during Beat 25 (Act 3, Level 6–7), concurrently with the three-crisis siege. This is an additional crisis, not a replacement. Directors should introduce it when the party has handled at least one siege crisis and is looking for what to address next.
Key NPCs: Söldís Glóðfari (Ember-Trader); Gunnar (first mate, three other crew).
Triggered By: Söldís flags the party down during the initial Beat 25 assessment, or the Director introduces it as a crisis opportunity.

Overview

Söldís Glóðfari arrived at Hrafnborg six days ago, ahead of the siege. She came with a merchant vessel loaded with emergency trade goods: ember-oil, fire-starting compressed fiber, thermal blankets, and enough preserved iron rations for forty people for two weeks.

She docked at the western harbor. She was unloading when the siege began. She got most of the people off. The cargo stayed. The undead came up the harbor road from the south and cut off access to the dock. Her wide-bottomed trading ship is stranded at the dock with four crew still aboard. The crew have been holding the dock for three days. They will not survive another night.

The cargo, if recovered, changes the siege calculus: ember-oil can be deployed against undead (the heat kills the rot-binding efficiently), the fire-starting supplies extend defensive perimeters, and the rations free up Harald's emergency reserves.

Director — Siege Second Chance (two rolls, take best): If the cargo is recovered, the party can deploy ember-oil in any one of the three Beat 25 crisis encounters, granting second chance (two rolls, take best) on all attack rolls in that scene as the oil creates a barrier of flame the undead struggle to cross.
Additionally, Harald notes the rations release his reserves for a strategic defensive decision — use this as a narrative beat (Harald making a choice he couldn't have made without the resources).

Part 1 — Söldís's Situation

Söldís is on the inner harbor wall, watching her vessel through a gap in the parapet with the expression of someone watching something they built burn slowly.

She is compact and dark-haired, with the callused hands of someone who has worked cargo holds their whole life. She speaks without turning from the harbor.

"Four crew. Three days. They've been signaling with a mirror — I can see them from here when the smoke shifts."

She turns.

"I know you have other things to do. I'm not asking you to save them for my sake. The cargo is 400 units of ember-oil and enough fire-fiber to burn a city gate. Whatever comes through the west wall, that's what kills it."

Söldís's briefing:

  • The dock: accessible via the harbor road (currently undead-held) OR via the water (cold, fast-moving, passable with swimming checks)
  • The vessel: secure — the crew have barricaded the cabin and hold; they can be reached
  • The cargo: still in the hold, intact — the undead haven't tried to destroy it
  • The undead on the dock: a sustained patrol, not a mass assault — approximately 8–12 individual draugr, rotating

Part 2 — Reaching the Vessel

Option A — Harbor Road

The harbor road runs along the inside of the western wall, accessible from the main gate approach. It is undead-held for the last quarter mile before the dock.

Scene: Harbor Road
Type: Combat (clearing a path)  |  Difficulty: Moderate
Enemies: 8 Draugr Troops + 1 Wight-Sentinel (Level 6 Leader-type — elevated commander unit)
The party does not need to kill everything. They need to clear a path to the dock — if they break the patrol line, the crew can move.

Option B — Water Approach

The harbor is reachable by cutting across the inner dockyard and entering the water at an unguarded point. Cold and fast — standard cold exposure rules apply — but bypasses the patrol entirely.

Skill Checks — Water Approach
Agility (Moderate, 12): Navigate the current without being swept past the dock. Failure: arrives two rounds late.
Might (Moderate, 12): Pull onto the dock platform in armor. Failure: drops prone at dock second chance (two rolls, take best) (recoverable).
The crew can lower a rope if signaled, reducing the Might check by 5.

Meeting the Crew

The four crew are alive: three sailors and first mate Gunnar (practical, shaken, grateful). They have food for another day. No weapons beyond ship tools — boarding hooks and a cargo hammer. They can help with cargo extraction if given direction. Treat them as a group resource: in one round they can transfer 100 units of ember-oil to wherever the party directs, provided they have safety to work.

Part 3 — The Cargo

Extracting the Ember-Oil

The hold is full. Moving 400 units of ember-oil requires either multiple trips with the crew helping (3 rounds of work, moderate combat pressure from draugr patrols) or one complete extraction via vessel movement (Gunnar qualifies to pilot it but will need the dock kept clear while he moves the ship).

Extraction Options
Partial Hit Die (1 round of combat + work): 100–150 units recovered — grants second chance (two rolls, take best) for one siege crisis.
Full Hit Die (3 rounds + cleared dock): Full cargo — second chance (two rolls, take best) on all combat in the crisis scene it is deployed in, plus Harald gains the strategic resource.

The Complication

As the cargo is being extracted, the Wight-Commander from the eastern gate redirects a secondary unit toward the harbor — someone has noticed activity at the dock. The party has limited time before a second wave arrives.

Gunnar, at the helm, does not wait to be told. He starts moving the ship — slowly, so slowly — off the dock and toward the inner harbor mooring, where there are walls between him and the road.

"Go," he says, to no one in particular. "I've got the cargo. You have the rest."

Part 4 — Resolution

milestones — Side Quest
2 VP: Söldís's crew alive, full or majority cargo recovered
1 VP: Crew alive, cargo partially lost or deployment limited
0 VP: Vessel abandoned
Award VP when the cargo is secured at the inner harbor mooring (out of undead access).

If the extraction is successful, Söldís meets the party at the inner harbor mooring.

"I've been moving ember-oil since I was fourteen years old. My mother said there was no future in it. She was talking about the market."

She begins organizing the distribution immediately, already giving orders to runners about where the oil goes and in what quantities.

She does not say thank you, exactly. She says: "Tell me what you need burned."

Söldís becomes a combat-useful resource for the rest of Beat 25 — she and Gunnar deploy the ember-oil according to the party's direction, effectively taking one crisis scene off the party's list by managing the fire-line themselves (Director discretion on which crisis).

Director Notes

Söldís's Character: She is a trader, not a warrior. She is also extremely good at logistics. Her value is the cargo, but her personality makes the hit die feel human rather than mechanical. Let her be competent and slightly wry.

The Crew: Gunnar is the most developed of the four. He can appear briefly in a later epilogue if the Director wants to close the loop — he made it out, he's already hired for the next supply run.

Skipping This SQ: The ember-oil is not deployed in Beat 25. The siege is harder — no additional second chance (two rolls, take best) available. Harald may make reference to the stranded vessel if the party is struggling, but no direct consequence beyond increased difficulty.

Beat 25 — The Last Flame

Director Guide — Three-Crisis Siege Montage, Söldís Arrival, Hildvarð's Heitfolk, Harald's Command

VP Status — Entering This Beat — Total VP (max): 65 | Level: 5 | VP into Level: 1
Level 5 begins - heroes levelled up completing Beat 24.

Overview

The party defends Hrafnborg against three simultaneous crises: the East Gate draugr tide, Rot Grubs undermining the western wall, and a Wight-Commander's battering ram at the main gate. This is an impossible triage — 4 heroes, 2 rounds, 8 tests total, but ~9 successes needed to hold all three crises. At least one will be neglected. The neglected crisis determines Grafvitnir's access in Beat 26.

Part 1 — Hrafnborg Assessment

The party is brought to Harald immediately. The King is on the walls — visible, organised, exhausted. He looks ten years older than Act 1. His beard is full of ash. His eyes are still clear but running on final reserves.

Harald's Briefing:

  • East Gate: Organised undead pressing in timed rotations. Gate braces beginning to splinter. Defenders rotating too fast, cold-bitten, exhausted.
  • West Wall: Reports from the tower basement — the stone sounds wrong. Hollow. Something is eating the foundation. The wall above is cracking. The whole tower might be compromised.
  • Main Gate: A Wight-Commander (draugr with intelligence) has assembled a bone-and-timber battering ram. Twelve draugr in synchronised movement. Two impacts already. Three more and the gate opens.
Note — Harald's Weight: Harald has held this city alone through the siege. He does not ask for sympathy — he asks for command structure. If the party hesitates, he waits patiently and repeats: "Tell me what to do." He will follow their orders because they are the heroes of this war, and he is the guardian of the walls.

Part 2 — Söldís Arrives

A cart arrives through a coastal route from the south — the last route before the siege ring tightens. Söldís Glóðfari drives it with complete conviction. She parks in the great hall courtyard, opens the back, and pulls out a clay pot — simple, sealed, ancient. The clay is cracked with age. The seal is wax and twine, wrapped so many times it has become sculptural.

She opens the pot. Inside: an ember. Twenty-two years old. Still burning. Still alive.

She sits next to the Ever-Ember shrine with the pot in her hands. She waits.

DIRECTOR SECRET — SÖLDÍS'S EMBER: The party does not know about Söldís's ember. No one told them she was coming. Lew never mentioned her. She is positioning herself for Beat 26. She has known, from the moment she lit her ember twenty-two years ago, that it would be needed for one specific moment. Do NOT telegraph her importance. Let the party see an eccentric old woman with a clay pot and wonder.

Part 3 — The Heitfolk Contingent & Hildvarð Bruneson

Hildvarð Bruneson emerges from the western tower basement — a deep-dwarf with grey-streaked hair, soot on face, and the competence of someone solving problems in bad conditions for a very long time. The Heitfolk arrived two days before the party through their tunnel system. Hildvarð has organised a defensive line, shoring up the foundation with Heitfolk stone-working expertise. But he cannot stop what is happening.

"The grubs came first. Rot-grubs, eating the foundation from inside. We've sealed what we can reach. But the deep tunnels run deeper than we can dig. Something followed the grubs. Something larger."

DIRECTOR SECRET — GRAFVITNIR'S ROUTE: The grubs are real. The tunnels are real. But Grafvitnir, rising from below, will follow these tunnels upward regardless of what the party does. The question is not whether he comes — he does. The question is how much the tunnels are widened by the party's neglect or narrowed by their effort. See Beat 26 for how the West Wall crisis result affects Grafvitnir's emergence speed.

Part 5 — Closing Transition

After Round 2 is complete, describe the final state of all three crises based on results. Then:

The sound from below the great hall stops. The silence is worse than the sound. The floor begins to crack. The Ever-Ember burns. Söldís is still there, clay pot in hands. The cracks spread from the center outward — in shapes that look almost like roots.

Beat 26 begins.

Key Narrative Themes

  1. Impossible triage: The party cannot win all three crises. They must choose. The consequences are immediate and visible in Beat 26.
  2. How outcomes feed Beat 26: The neglected crises become Grafvitnir's access points.
  3. Söldís's quiet heroism: She is not fighting. She is positioning herself for the moment when the flame goes out and must be restored.
  4. Defending a real place: Hrafnborg is not abstract — it is full of people. The party is defending all of them.

Beat 26 — Grafvitnir Rises

Three-stage boss scene. Campaign climax. 10–12 rounds across 2–3 sessions.

Opening Based on Montage Outcomes

Choose the read-aloud version matching Beat 25 results:

Version A — All Three Crises Held (3+ successes each): The floor splits. Roots punch through like fists. Grafvitnir comes through the floor all at once, too fast, too large. Wolf-shaped, the size of a longhouse. Does not acknowledge the party. Goes immediately for the shrine.

Version B — One Crisis Failed, Two Held: The cracks are narrow. Grafvitnir emerges slower — forced to compress through tight passages. Thirty seconds of watching him force himself through the hole. Frustration in his growl. The passages were not wide enough.

Version C — Multiple Crises Failed (0–1 in two or more): The floor SHATTERS. The central section collapses inward. Roots erupt with such force they strike the ceiling. Grafvitnir ERUPTS — no hesitation, no squeezing. The party has seconds before he reaches the shrine.

Key Mechanic — Undying Hunger: Regains 5 Toughness per turn unless the Ever-Ember is burning in the same zone. The flame IS the weapon.

Key Mechanic — Spectral Instability: Reduces all incoming damage by 3 (min 1). Predictable soak — the party always knows.

Key Narrative Moments (Stage 2)

Lew Arrives (when things look dire):

The north gate opens. Lew runs through — behind him: Heitfolk, Green Heart elves, survivors from the settlements. His amber eye blazing. He shouts: "She's growing! Half an hour, maybe less!" He looks at Grafvitnir for two seconds. Then at the shrine. Then at the party. "What do you need me to do?" Then he is moving.

Grafvitnir Extinguishes the Flame: One thin, precise tendril wraps around the shrine. The flame goes out. The hall goes dark — all at once. The cold comes in immediately. Not weather-cold. The cold of a door into somewhere that has never been warm.

Söldís's Act:

Director Secret: This is the emotional climax. Söldís opens the clay pot she has carried for twenty-two years. She tilts it over the shrine. The flame IGNITES — fills the shrine, rises up the walls, warm and huge and unmistakably alive in the way only something kept alive by absolute refusal can be. Söldís sets the pot down. She does not look upset. She looks like a woman who has completed a task she began decades ago.

Stage Transition: Grafvitnir's howl shakes the city. He grows. Roots pull more mass from the ground. The ceiling breaks apart — sky visible above. He is no longer any shape. Through the hole: the Green Heart, blazing, growing stronger. For the first time, Grafvitnir is uncertain.

The Sealing

Light goes white — clean, absolute, unstoppable. Roots RECOIL. Grafvitnir is simply pulled downward. Cracks seal from the inside out. Undead outside stop — like puppets when the hand opens. The unnatural cold retreats. Natural Svellheim winter remains. The Ever-Ember burns. Kaelen's work burns in the east. Hrafnborg is warm.

Post-Combat Aftermath

Give the party space. The great hall is devastated but whole. The Ever-Ember burns gold and steady. Söldís sits by the shrine, empty clay pot in hand, smiling and crying. Lew trembles, looking east toward the Green Heart — toward Kaelen. Harald watches from the walls as the siege lines break apart.

Note: This is the climax of the entire campaign. Let the party sit with this victory. Answer questions. Let them interact with NPCs. Let the weight of what they've done settle. They have won.

The Morning After

The siege is over. The outer wards are littered with fallen undead — now just bodies returning to the stillness they should have kept. People are emerging from shelters. Someone is laughing. Fires are being built — not siege-fires, ordinary warming fires.

Tone Note: This beat is about aftermath and the specific texture of victory that doesn't feel like victory until you stop and look around. Stage whichever vignettes feel right for the table — none are mandatory.

NPC Vignettes

Harald: The King on the Walls

Harald stands at the Ever-Ember shrine. Not praying — just standing there. Looks at the flame, the scorch-marks, the whole flagstones. After a long time, he nods once. Then turns away and starts working.

If the party speaks with him: "You held. The city held. That's what matters." About Kaelen: "Then she's not lost to us. She's doing what needs to be done."

Note: Harald will govern well for the rest of his life because he has now seen exactly what Hrafnborg is worth defending.

Söldís: The Ember-Trader at Rest

Her clay pot sits on a shelf, empty, ordinary. She sits next to it one morning. Then she gets up, loads her cart, assembles her caravan. Three new people from Hrafnborg join her.

If the party speaks with her: "I did what needed doing. My flame and the Ever-Ember — they belonged together. Now they are." She offers each party member a small carved token. "You held when holding was the only thing that mattered."

Director Secret: For the first time in twenty-two years, Söldís travels light. Her caravan grows — not with embers, but with people and goods and news. Her arc is complete.

The Green Heart: Kaelen's New Home

If the party travels east: the grove is changed. Trees lit gold from within. Snow melts within fifty feet of the central tree — clear bare ground where grass might grow in spring.

Grénvörðr: "She is learning. To be a flame. To be a root. To be both and neither. Do not mistake silence for absence."

The central tree's light pulses in response to the party's presence. Not speech — communication in warmth, rhythm, flame-pulse: I remember you.

On Yggdrasil: "The Rot is still there. Below her. But Yggdrasil is no longer dying. She anchors it. She feeds it light. In three hundred years, perhaps longer, Yggdrasil might heal."

Lew: The Wanderer's Return

Lew stays in Hrafnborg for several days. Eats, sleeps, helps with wall repairs. His amber eye glows steadier. His attention turns east.

One evening, a party member finds him on the north wall, looking east. After a few days, he's gone. Left his spare pack, his cooking knife, and a note: "Tell them I came back."

Director Secret — Lew at the Green Heart: If the party follows, they find him kneeling at the inner grove. One hand on the roots. Both eyes open — one dark, one amber and blazing. Speaking quietly. A vow, or a promise. When he stands, he touches the eye once — gently, the way you'd touch a scar that saved your life — and nods. The tree-light pulses in answer. The arc completes: the Keeper-marked man reaching the woman he was always going to guide to this place.

The Settlements: Hit Die Begins

Settlements the party helped are now recovering. Supplies moving between settlements and the city. Trade beginning. The world starting to believe it might not die after all.

Thyra Blackhand: "You did what you said you would. You gave us a reason to believe."

Final Image

Read the final read-aloud. On quiet nights, from the north wall, people see a light moving in the Green Heart. Not a torch — steady, following no path, never hurries. It pulses. Like a heartbeat answering another heartbeat across a room. Nobody has gone to look for it. Lew has.

Campaign Reflection (Optional)

If the table wants a debrief: questions about the world, NPCs, what-ifs. Reflection on the journey. Discussion of themes. Moments that mattered.

Note: The party did not save the world by becoming extraordinary. They saved it by being the vehicle and protection — getting the right people to the right place, defending the things that needed defending, holding the line when holding was all that was left. Let them sit with that.
